query: Issue #412 is groomed and ready. Spin up an agent to implement it and open a PR.
note: Spawning one implementation sub-agent from a ready issue — the core dispatch act.

query: Send an agent at #88 to fix the date-parsing bug.
note: "send an agent at #N" is explicitly called out as a trigger phrase even without the word dispatch.

query: Go fix these three bugs: #101, #102, #103. They touch different files.
note: File-disjoint issues to implement via agents — selection + parallel dispatch, not a loop request.

query: The agent I sent on #57 got watchdog-killed and returned status:failed. What now?
note: Stalled-agent worktree salvage vs re-dispatch decision lives here.

query: My sub-agent says it finished #200 and opened the PR. Should I believe it?
note: Verify-then-trust completion checks on a reported-complete dispatch.

query: Write the brief for an agent to refactor the auth module per issue PROJ-19, with a worktree and pre-push hygiene.
note: The eight-section dispatch brief (worktree, toolchain prefix, pre-push hygiene) is this skill's core.

query: Have a coding agent knock out the typo fixes in the settings page — they're all tiny.
note: Casual; bundling same-surface small fixes into one dispatch is a selection rule here.

query: Issue #310 has agent-model:opus set. Spawn the implementer at the right model and effort.
note: Model/effort resolution from chat > labels > defaults at dispatch time.

query: That agent on #44 stalled with commits in its worktree — salvage it instead of starting over.
note: Stalled-agent worktree audit and salvage path.

query: Build me a quick implementation for #5 — just one agent, nothing fancy.
note: Single-agent code implementation from a tracker issue is exactly this skill.

query: Keep 6 agents going on the backlog and refill a slot whenever one merges.
note: The N-slot fill/refill loop is orchestrating-slots, not a single dispatch.

query: Work the queue P0-first and react to merge events as they come in.
note: "Work the queue" + event-driven refill is the orchestration loop, not one dispatch.

query: PR #77 is green but won't merge — auto-merge seems to have mysteriously dropped.
note: Post-open PR lifecycle (auto-merge re-arm) — the agent already opened the PR.

query: CI is red on the PR my agent opened. Triage the failing job before we touch code.
note: CI failure triage on an already-open PR belongs to driving-prs-to-merge despite the agent mention.

query: Before I send any agent at it, #260 is still vague — help me tighten the story with the lead.
note: Issue not dev-ready; grooming the WHAT/WHY precedes dispatch — keyword 'agent' is a lure.

query: Recon #150 and tell me the implementation approach and LoE before we dispatch anyone.
note: Read-only recon to size/approach a groomed issue — distinct dev-side pass before dispatch.

query: Lock #99 and set agent-claimed before anyone picks it up.
note: The claim/lock protocol itself is gh-issue-locking; dispatch only consumes it.

query: Which model tier should agent-model:* map to, and why is there no cheap-tier effort setting?
note: Definition of the control-field taxonomy is gh-issue-labels, not the act of dispatching.

query: Turn this stack trace the user pasted into a groomed, dispatchable GitHub issue.
note: Filing/grooming an issue to dispatchable shape is issue-filing, upstream of dispatch.

query: Our flight booking app keeps double-charging passengers on the return leg — can you reproduce it?
note: Truly unrelated: an app-behavior reproduction request, no agent/issue orchestration involved.
